← all tutorials

Three-Phase Commit (3PC) — interactive 📖 THREE_PHASE_COMMIT.md 📄 three_phase_commit.py

📖 Full guide  ·  📄 Ground-truth source — every panel below recomputes the same model in JS.
A coordinator shepherds a transaction through CanCommit? → PreCommit → DoCommit. Pick where the coordinator crashes and watch the participants run the termination protocol and finish without blocking. Every number is recomputed live in JS from the identical protocol as three_phase_commit.py.

① The three phases & crash recovery — step through a 3PC run

step 0 / 0
CanCommit? (Phase 1) PreCommit (Phase 2) DoCommit (Phase 3) Abort / crash termination protocol
How to read: arrows sweep left↔right between the Coordinator and Participants. Each box shows its live state (INIT→READY→PRECOMMITTED→COMMITTED). When the coordinator crashes (red ✗), the termination protocol takes over: participants pool states and decide by the four Skeen rules. No participant ever blocks — it always reaches a final state.

② 2PC vs 3PC — the blocking window, and what the extra round trip buys

What this shows: 3PC costs +1 round trip (and +50% messages) on the happy path. The payoff: when the coordinator crashes while participants are READY, 2PC BLOCKS (a participant can't safely commit or abort) while 3PC decides. Drag N to scale the bars.
Round-trips: 2PC = 2, 3PC = 3. Messages: 2PC = 4·N, 3PC = 6·N. The extra PreCommit phase is the entire reason 3PC is non-blocking — it splits the ambiguous READY state into "uncertain" vs "safe-to-commit".

③ The four termination rules (Skeen 1981)

After a coordinator crash, participants pool states and decide. Pick a crash point to see which rule fires and the resulting uniform final state.
ruleparticipant states observeddecisionwhy
(1)any COMMITTEDCOMMITa final commit already happened
(2)any ABORTEDABORTa final abort already happened
(3)all PRECOMMITTEDCOMMITeveryone voted YES → commit was decided
(4)otherwise (some READY)ABORTcan't guarantee unanimous YES → play safe
check: … JS 3PC recompute matches three_phase_commit.py GOLD (crash after PreCommit→COMMIT, before→ABORT, all uniform, never blocks).
